mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL values directly increase inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and compress valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of months when CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals impending corporate margin compression and reduced consumer spending, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST indicates robust consumer and housing demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (F-test p > 0.10) over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO values during a monetary tightening cycle reflect supply-side constraints that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the regime split shows no significant difference in predictive power (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Increases in PAYEMS reflect expanding employment and rising household income, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns over a 2-year rolling window, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling FEDFUNDS and rising GDPC1 signals accommodative policy supporting economic growth, which expands equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The dual signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS drops below its 6-month moving average, consumer spending contraction triggers immediate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive in >65% of months when RSAFS falls below its 6-month average, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO during periods of low UNRATE indicates capacity utilization approaching maximums, which forces firms to delay capex and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases when unemployment is below 4.5% yield positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the regime-conditional predictive accuracy drops below random chance.
